Formulas.Description: The structural frame a ship is built on. It has its own mass (Hull Mass — the frame's weight, which every hulled ship carries) and it sets the mass budget the ship's mounted components must fit within. The space echo of a ground unit's chassis. Template defaults are the MEDIUM tier; the light/heavy tiers override these dials per design.

Properties.Name: Hull Mass
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The frame''s own weight — every ship carries it, so a bigger hull is heavier (and dodges worse). Decoupled from the budget below.'

Properties.Name: Mass Budget
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The mass ceiling that a ship''s mounted components must fit within (a bigger hull holds more).'

Formulas.Description: A large-scale mining operation with vehicles, structures, and extensive infrastructure for resource extraction.

Properties.Name: Area
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Area covered by this mining operation in square meters'

Formulas.Description: An unmanned, transportable resources collector
Research new technologies

Properties.Name: Cost Per Day
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Base cost to operate the research lab per day'

Properties.Name: Research Specialty
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Provides a 10 percent bonus towards research in the specialty category. May still research other categories without the bonus.'

Properties.Name: RP Amount Per EconTick
Properties.PropertyFormula: AtbConstrArgs(PropertyValue('Research Points'), PropertyValue('Cost Per Day'), PropertyValue('Research Specialty'))

Formulas.Description: An installation designed to refine base minerals into materials
Constructs Facilities, Fighters, Ammo and Components

Properties.Name: Size
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Size of the factory, larger factories produce more construction points'

Formulas.Description: Construction and launch facilities for ships

Properties.Name: Slip Size
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Maximum gross tonnage this slip can work on'

Properties.Name: Crew Size
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'More crew leads to faster build times'

Formulas.Description: Handles import and Export of goods

Properties.Name: Logistic Capacity
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How many different items this office can handle'

Formulas.Description: Stores Fuel

Properties.Name: Tank Radius
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The radius of this tank in m'

Formulas.Description: Stores Fuel

Properties.Name: Dry Weight
Properties.PropertyFormula: (1.333 * Pi * ( Pow(PropertyValue('Tank Radius'),3) - Pow( PropertyValue('Tank Radius') - 0.004, 3)  ) * 8000)

Formulas.Description: Graduates Naval officers

Properties.Name: Class Size
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How big the graduating class size of the academy will be each year.'

Properties.Name: Class Length
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How many months it takes to graduate a class. Longer training graduates officers with better experience and potential.'

Formulas.Description: Graduates specialist scientists. A longer program graduates scientists with a higher research-competence ceiling in the academy's specialty.

Properties.Name: Class Size
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How many scientists graduate from the academy each cycle.'

Properties.Name: Class Length
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How many months it takes to graduate a class. Longer training graduates scientists with a higher research-competence ceiling.'

Properties.Name: Research Specialty
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The tech category the academy trains its scientists in. Graduates carry a research-competence bonus in this category.'

Properties.Name: DBargs
Properties.PropertyFormula: AtbConstrArgs(PropertyValue('Class Size'), PropertyValue('Class Length'), PropertyValue('Research Specialty'))

Formulas.Description: The seat of your spy network. Sets how many concurrent covert operations your agents can run and your counter-intelligence rating against enemy agents operating on your soil.

Properties.Name: Op Capacity
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How many concurrent covert operations your agents can run.'

Properties.Name: Counter Intel Rating
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Your defensive strength against enemy agents operating on your soil.'

Formulas.Description: A command seat from which a posted leader works a field-site (an anomaly, a ruin). Its Role sets which kind of site it can work; a higher Grade works the site faster; Survivability protects the leader at a dangerous posting.

Properties.Name: Role
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Which kind of field-site this berth can work: a Science berth works a science anomaly, a Tactical berth a combat site, and so on.'

Properties.Name: Grade
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The berth quality tier. A higher grade works a field-site faster.'

Properties.Name: Support
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'A flat competence boost to the leader posted in this berth.'

Properties.Name: Survivability
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How well the berth protects its posted leader at a dangerous site — reduces the chance of an incident that injures or kills them.'

Properties.Name: Span
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The force size this berth can command at the site (matters once sites are fought over).'

Formulas.Description: A generic does everything space facility

Properties.Name: Size Efficiency
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The amount of tonnage taken up by racking, office space etc.'

Properties.Name: Cargo Transfer Rate
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How much cargo can be transfered per secong in kg.'

Properties.Name: Transfer Range
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How much Dv can be expended to transfer cargo'

Properties.Name: Total Cargo Stored
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The volume of cargo this can store.'
Properties.PropertyFormula: PropertyValue('Warehouse Size') - PropertyValue('Size Efficiency') - PropertyValue('Cargo Transfer Rate')

Formulas.Description: The underlying support a colony needs to function: power, roads, utilities and housing. Provides the support capacity that every other installation on the body draws on, and houses colonists on hostile worlds.

Properties.Name: Support Colonists
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Population supported when the body's gravity and atmosphere fall within tolerance. Out-of-tolerance bodies receive no support from this design.'

Properties.Name: Storage Amount
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The amount of space that is dedicated to storage space. '

Properties.Name: Support Capacity
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Support capacity each unit of this infrastructure provides to the colony. Other installations consume this capacity; if demand exceeds supply all colony output is reduced. Only counts on bodies within the gravity and pressure tolerances below.'

Properties.Name: Housing Comfort
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Quality-of-life comfort this infrastructure provides — a capped morale bonus on top of bare life-support. Basic utilities and housing keep colonists content, not just alive.'

Properties.Name: Min Gravity
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Surface gravity range (m/s²) this infrastructure can operate in. Earth standard is 9.81 m/s². Drag the left/right handles to set the bounds; drag the bar between them to shift the whole range.'

Properties.Name: Max Gravity
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Upper bound of the gravity operating range. Configured via the Min Gravity range slider.'

Properties.Name: Min Pressure
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Atmospheric pressure range (Earth atmospheres) this infrastructure can operate in. Drag the handles to set the bounds; drag the bar between them to shift the whole range.'

Properties.Name: Max Pressure
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Upper bound of the pressure operating range. Configured via the Min Pressure range slider.'

Formulas.Description: A sealed, self-contained habitat module. Provides life support and housing for station crews regardless of the body the station orbits — it carries its own internal environment, so unlike planetary infrastructure it has NO gravity or pressure tolerance limits and supports population anywhere (asteroid, dwarf, deep space).

Properties.Name: Support Colonists
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Population this sealed habitat supports — anywhere, since it provides its own environment (no gravity or atmosphere tolerance applies).'

Properties.Name: Storage Amount
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'The amount of space that is dedicated to storage space. '

Properties.Name: Support Capacity
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Support capacity each unit of this habitat provides. Other installations on the station consume this capacity.'

Properties.Name: Housing Comfort
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Quality-of-life comfort this sealed habitat provides — a capped morale bonus for the station crew.'

Formulas.Description: Facilities for transfering cargo

Properties.Name: Cargo Transfer Rate
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How much cargo can be transfered per second in kg.'

Properties.Name: Transfer Range
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How much Dv can be expended to transfer cargo'

Formulas.Description: Office space for bureaucracy
Provides construction points for construction projects at this colony

Properties.Name: Level
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Level of construction services provided, each level provides additional construction points per day.'

Formulas.Description: A launch facility for putting ships into orbit. Each installation provides one launch pad.

Properties.Name: Max Tonnage
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Maximum ship mass this launch pad can handle (kg)'

Formulas.Description: A hardened defensive emplacement. Fortifies the region it stands in and projects cover to adjacent friendly regions — the ground-combat counter to an invasion (a Bunker fortifies; a solar panel does not).

Properties.Name: LocalFortify
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How much this bunker fortifies its own region (0..1 = +% defence).'

Properties.Name: AdjacentProjection
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How much it projects to each adjacent friendly region.'

Properties.Name: TileFootprint
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How many fine city-tiles this base occupies once the city sub-grid is built.'

Properties.Name: groundFootprintAtbArgs
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'double tileFootprint — makes the bunker a strategic presence on the operational war map (a capture/bombard target).'

Formulas.Description: Boots on the ground - cheap, holds terrain, the baseline of any invasion or defence. Fights up close (1 hex). Building it raises an Infantry unit on the planet.

Properties.Name: Attack
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Damage this unit deals per salvo to enemies in range.'

Properties.Name: Defense
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Reduces damage this unit takes (soaks incoming fire).'

Properties.Name: HitPoints
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How much damage this unit can absorb before it is destroyed.'

Properties.Name: Range
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Strike range in hexes (how far it can hit; artillery reaches farther).'

Properties.Name: Penetration
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Armour penetration - how much of an enemy''s armour this unit ignores. Infantry small arms bounce off heavy plate (0).'

Properties.Name: PerShotEnergy
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Per-shot size - how much of this unit''s attack lands in ONE shot. Small arms put many little rounds downrange (10), so they chip and mostly bounce off armour.'

Properties.Name: groundUnitAtbArgs
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'double unitType, double attack, double defense, double hitPoints, double range, double penetration, double perShotEnergy'
Properties.PropertyFormula: AtbConstrArgs(PropertyValue('UnitType'), PropertyValue('Attack'), PropertyValue('Defense'), PropertyValue('HitPoints'), PropertyValue('Range'), PropertyValue('Penetration'), PropertyValue('PerShotEnergy'))

Formulas.Description: Shock armour - strong in the open, weaker in rough terrain, tough to kill. Fights up close (1 hex). Building it raises an Armor unit on the planet.

Properties.Name: Attack
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Damage this unit deals per salvo to enemies in range.'

Properties.Name: Defense
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Reduces damage this unit takes (soaks incoming fire).'

Properties.Name: HitPoints
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How much damage this unit can absorb before it is destroyed.'

Properties.Name: Range
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Strike range in hexes (how far it can hit; artillery reaches farther).'

Properties.Name: Penetration
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Armour penetration - a tank''s AP main gun cracks plate that bounces small arms (ignores this much of an enemy''s Defense).'

Properties.Name: PerShotEnergy
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Per-shot size - a tank''s main gun lands its whole attack in ONE big shell (140), an alpha that punches flat armour instead of chipping at it.'

Properties.Name: groundUnitAtbArgs
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'double unitType, double attack, double defense, double hitPoints, double range, double penetration, double perShotEnergy'
Properties.PropertyFormula: AtbConstrArgs(PropertyValue('UnitType'), PropertyValue('Attack'), PropertyValue('Defense'), PropertyValue('HitPoints'), PropertyValue('Range'), PropertyValue('Penetration'), PropertyValue('PerShotEnergy'))

Formulas.Description: Reach - hits hard from 3 hexes away and can shoot while repositioning, but fragile if the enemy closes. Building it raises an Artillery unit on the planet.

Properties.Name: Attack
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Damage this unit deals per salvo to enemies in range.'

Properties.Name: Defense
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Reduces damage this unit takes (soaks incoming fire).'

Properties.Name: HitPoints
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'How much damage this unit can absorb before it is destroyed.'

Properties.Name: Range
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Strike range in hexes (how far it can hit; artillery reaches farther).'

Properties.Name: Penetration
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Armour penetration - shaped-charge / HE shells shrug off some plating (ignores this much of an enemy''s Defense), between infantry and a tank''s AP gun.'

Properties.Name: PerShotEnergy
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Per-shot size - artillery fires a few big HE shells (80 each), between a tank''s single alpha and an infantry spray - each shell is a real hit against armour.'

Properties.Name: groundUnitAtbArgs
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'double unitType, double attack, double defense, double hitPoints, double range, double penetration, double perShotEnergy'
Properties.PropertyFormula: AtbConstrArgs(PropertyValue('UnitType'), PropertyValue('Attack'), PropertyValue('Defense'), PropertyValue('HitPoints'), PropertyValue('Range'), PropertyValue('Penetration'), PropertyValue('PerShotEnergy'))

Formulas.Description: A single soldier's body — the baseline frame. Low carry strength (a rifle, not an autocannon) until augmented; add power armour to lug heavier gear.
A standard infantry rifle — light enough for any frame to shoulder.
Body armour — trades weight for a bigger health pool and damage mitigation.

Ablative laminate armour (Enhancers/Defence: armour tuned against a damage NATURE). The outer layer boils away under coherent energy, so it shrugs off lasers and plasma — but the light laminate is thin against a solid kinetic slug that punches straight through. Same mass and mitigation as composite plating; what differs is WHAT it stops. Balance dials flagged.

Properties.Name: VsKinetic
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Soak effectiveness vs KINETIC (1.0 = plain plate). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: VsEnergy
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Soak effectiveness vs ENERGY (1.0 = plain plate). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: VsExplosive
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Soak effectiveness vs EXPLOSIVE (1.0 = plain plate). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: VsExotic
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Soak effectiveness vs EXOTIC (1.0 = plain plate). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: ablativePlatingArgs
Properties.PropertyFormula: AtbConstrArgs(PropertyValue('CarryMass'), PropertyValue('HP'), PropertyValue('Defense'), PropertyValue('VsKinetic'), PropertyValue('VsEnergy'), PropertyValue('VsExplosive'), PropertyValue('VsExotic'))

Formulas.Description: Reactive armour (Defence: armour tuned against a damage NATURE). Explosive-filled blocks detonate outward when struck, defeating a shaped-charge/HE warhead and deflecting a solid slug — so it excels against KINETIC and EXPLOSIVE fire. But a coherent energy beam doesn't trigger the reactive charge and burns straight through the light casing, so it is POOR against energy. The kinetic/explosive counterpart to ablative laminate. Same mass and mitigation as composite; what differs is WHAT it stops. Balance dials flagged.

Properties.Name: VsKinetic
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Soak effectiveness vs KINETIC (1.0 = plain plate). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: VsEnergy
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Soak effectiveness vs ENERGY (1.0 = plain plate). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: VsExplosive
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Soak effectiveness vs EXPLOSIVE (1.0 = plain plate). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: VsExotic
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Soak effectiveness vs EXOTIC (1.0 = plain plate). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: reactivePlatingArgs
Properties.PropertyFormula: AtbConstrArgs(PropertyValue('CarryMass'), PropertyValue('HP'), PropertyValue('Defense'), PropertyValue('VsKinetic'), PropertyValue('VsEnergy'), PropertyValue('VsExplosive'), PropertyValue('VsExotic'))

Formulas.Description: Servo-assisted armour — its strength boost (+300) lets a human frame carry weapons it never could bare-handed (the Space Marine story), and it toughens the wearer.
A heavy support weapon — too heavy for a bare human frame to shoulder (needs a strong frame or power armour), but devastating.
An armoured tracked chassis — the tank/vehicle frame. Huge carry strength (mounts heavy guns a human never could) and hauled by a VEHICLE bay, not a troop bay.
A vehicle main gun — long reach and heavy hitting, far too heavy for any infantry frame.
A striding war-machine chassis (a mech) — big carry strength for heavy weapons, tough, and it walks (the mech/Titan frame; scale it up for a Titan).
A directed-energy weapon (plasma/laser) — clean mid-weight punch at range, the energy-damage flavour.
A personal energy shield — a flat pool of incoming damage it soaks before HP is touched (survive-by-shield; drops once depleted).

A fast-cycling shield ward (⚙3 Defence: the capacity-vs-recharge decision). It holds a SMALLER pool than a big Shield Generator, but recharges much faster between salvos — so it shrugs off sustained chip-fire (the pool is back before the next volley) where a big slow shield eats one alpha then stays down. The trade is burst-durability vs one-hit-buffer. Recharge + capacity dials flagged for a balance pass.

Properties.Name: Shield
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Flat incoming-damage soak pool (smaller than a big generator). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: ShieldRegenFraction
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Fraction of the pool restored per hour between salvos (0.34 = the standard generator). FLAGGED balance value.'

Properties.Name: wardProjectorArgs
Properties.PropertyFormula: AtbConstrArgs(PropertyValue('CarryMass'), PropertyValue('StrengthBonus'), PropertyValue('EvasionBonus'), PropertyValue('ToughnessBonus'), PropertyValue('Shield'), PropertyValue('ShieldRegenFraction'))

Formulas.Description: Reflex-enhancing wetware/servos — raises the unit's chance to simply avoid a hit (survive-by-dodge; useless against saturation fire).
A tiny, cheap, fragile organism/drone frame — the swarm body. Almost no HP and low carry strength, but nearly free to build a thousand of (pair with the Count 'bunch').

Properties.Name: Size
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Bulk (feeds transport carry-size) — tiny, so a bay holds a horde.'

Formulas.Description: Natural/close-combat weapons — weigh almost nothing but reach only the same hex (melee, range 0). The swarm's teeth.
A mass-based ammunition magazine - feeds the ammo weapons (flak, railgun) a ground unit carries. Bigger holds more before it runs dry and must resupply.

A DESIGNABLE drive you mount on a ground unit - tweak the dials to build any locomotion for any environment (a cheap fast wheel for open plains, an all-terrain track for the mountains, an amphibious skimmer for a water world). Foot is the 1.0 baseline; higher speed, better rough-terrain handling, and amphibious ability each add mass and cost.

Properties.Name: SpeedFactor
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'March-speed multiplier (1.0 = Foot baseline; higher = faster = pricier and heavier). The BENEFIT dial.'

Properties.Name: RoughHandling
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Rough-terrain handling, 0..1 (0 = wheels, slowed hard in rough; 1 = tracks/walker, crosses broken ground evenly). Costs more the higher it is. The all-terrain dial.'

Properties.Name: Amphibious
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Can it cross water? 0 = no, 1 = yes. Roughly doubles the mass/cost - the water-world option.'

Formulas.Description: A ground radar you mount on a unit to REVEAL THE MAP. Each tick the carrying unit sees and surveys the ground within its detection range - the way to explore a fogged world without waiting for a geo-survey. Longer range sees farther but costs more mass. Mount it on a fast, low-support chassis to make a scout.

Properties.Name: Range
Properties.DescriptionFormula: 'Detection range in real kilometres - how far the radar sees on the ground (translated to hexes on the planet map, since a hex is bigger on Earth than on Io).'
